status: targeted deterministic provenance validation; not TESS flight-data evidence
validated_checks: little-endian and big-endian float arrays produce the same canonical hash
distinct IEEE-754 NaN payloads produce the same canonical hash
array shape and individual numeric values change the hash
JSON object key order does not change metadata hashes
missing flux uncertainty is committed through an explicit sentinel
campaign-input hashes are computed after LightCurve finite-value filtering, positive-uncertainty filtering, and time sorting

observed_behavior: stored campaign-input hash equals a fresh hash of constructed arrays
stored campaign-input hash differs from the pre-construction temporary-array hash

limitations: The validation uses deterministic fixtures, not downloaded TESS products.
Hash equality proves identity under the declared canonicalization, not scientific correctness.
The full repository CI and six-target TESS workflow remain required before merge.
